programme.title: Innov'Industry 2026 Programme
programme.subtitle: Two days of conferences, track stands, interview simulations, and networking.

day1.location: Guest welcome · Badge distribution · Program handout

day1.title: Opening ceremony
day1.location: Prof. IJJAALI Mustapha · Prof. LAHRACH Abderrahim · Prof. HIHI Hicham · Prof. MOUSTABCHIR Hassane · Organizing committee representative

day1.location: Track showcases · Student exchanges · Winners · Partners

day2.title: Day 2 opening & welcome
day2.location: Participant welcome · Program recap · Company forum open all day

day2.title: Conference 5
day2.location: Entrepreneurship · Innovation & industrial entrepreneurship · 20 min talk · 10 min Q&A

day2.title: Innov’Dom semi-final
day2.location: Pitches by qualified teams · 2h presentations + 30 min results

day2.title: Semi-final results & lunch

day2.title: Awards & closing
day2.location: Results · Trophies · Certificates · Official closing speech

filieres.title: The 4 engineering tracks
filieres.subtitle: Clear positioning of skills and career outcomes for each track.

items.name: Energy Engineering and Intelligent Systems
items.summary: Renewables, smart energy supervision, and control engineering.

items.name: Mechanical Engineering
items.summary: Design, manufacturing, maintenance, and mechanical optimization.

items.name: Industrial Engineering
items.summary: Production systems, quality, supply chain, and performance management.

items.name: Mechatronics Engineering
items.summary: Embedded systems, robotics, instrumentation, and system integration.

competition.title: Innov'Dom Challenge — 2nd Edition
competition.subtitle: As part of Innov'Industry 2026, the Industrial Engineering Department at ENSA Fès, in collaboration with the PowerUp, MTZ, and IEC clubs, is organizing the 2nd edition of the Innov'Dom Challenge technology competition. This two-day event brings together the Energy, Mechanics, Mechatronics, and Industrial Engineering tracks around technological innovation.
competition.eligibility: Students (Bachelor, Master, Engineering), PhD candidates & researchers, start-ups & professionals. Individual or team participation (max 4 members). Academic supervisor allowed — consultative role only.
competition.prizeDescription: Awards presented during the Innov'Industry 2026 closing ceremony.
criteria: Clarity and quality of the presentation
Mastery of the topic during Q&A
Quality of the prototype or demo

timeline.text: Dossier submission · Deadline: April 5, 2026 · Results: April 10, 2026

clubs.name: Power Up
clubs.description: Power Up trains students on rapid prototyping, project delivery, and applied innovation for energy systems.

clubs.name: Mechatronics Trendz
clubs.description: Trendz supports students on mechatronic systems, embedded robotics, and control engineering.

clubs.name: IEC 4.0
clubs.description: IEC 4.0 promotes continuous improvement culture, industrial data use, and performance management practices.
